title: Add streak counter tracking consecutive days of database module usage
description: Duolingo's streaks increase Day-7 retention by 14% (Duolingo blog). Zero retention features exist in the module. Add: (1) Daily streak counter persisted to localStorage, (2) Streak display in module header, (3) Streak freeze: allow 1 missed day without losing streak, (4) Visual celebration at milestones (7 days, 30 days). The streak checks if the user interacted with ANY database mode (insert, search, analyze, etc.) — not just opened the page. Source: Duolingo streak mechanics, loss aversion psychology.
acceptanceCriteria: Streak counter visible in Database module header showing consecutive days
Streak increments when user performs at least 1 meaningful interaction per day
Streak freeze: 1 allowed gap day per 7-day period without breaking streak
Visual celebration at 7, 14, 30, 60, 100 day milestones
Streak persisted to localStorage with date stamps
Streak resets to 0 after 2+ missed days (without freeze)

title: Add daily database challenge (new problem each day)
description: A new mini-challenge each day to give users a reason to return. Types: (1) 'What NF is this schema?' — normalization recall, (2) 'Predict: will this query use an index?' — query plan prediction, (3) 'Which isolation level prevents phantom reads?' — transaction recall, (4) 'How many splits to insert [5,3,8,1,9,2] into order-3 B-tree?' — B-tree analysis. Pool of 100+ questions, one served per day based on date hash. Source: Duolingo daily goals.
acceptanceCriteria: Daily Challenge banner visible on module load showing today's challenge
Challenge changes every day (deterministic based on date, not random)
Pool of at least 50 questions across all 7 modes
Completing daily challenge awards 20 XP and maintains streak
Challenge result persisted — can't redo same day's challenge
'See answer' button with explanation after attempting

title: Add per-concept mastery tracking with visual skill map
description: Track mastery per database concept: ER Diagram ★★☆☆☆, Normalization ★★★☆☆, B-Tree ★☆☆☆☆, etc. Stars earned through: viewing (1★), interacting (2★), predicting correctly >60% (3★), completing comparison challenges (4★), acing debugging challenges (5★). Visual skill map in sidebar or header showing all concepts with their star levels. Weakness identification: 'Suggested: practice Hash Index (lowest mastery)'. Source: Brilliant.org mastery tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 7+ database modes has a 0-5 star mastery rating
Stars earned progressively: view=1★, interact=2★, predict>60%=3★, compare=4★, debug=5★
Skill map visible in sidebar showing all concept mastery levels
Weakness indicator: highlights lowest-mastery concept with 'Practice this' prompt
Mastery persisted to localStorage
Visual celebration when concept reaches 5★

title: Add share button with URL-encoded visualization state
description: Zero sharing capability exists. Add: (1) Share button in each mode, (2) Clicking encodes current state into URL params: mode, entities (for ER), keys (for B-tree), steps (for hash), etc., (3) Opening the URL restores exact state, (4) Copy to clipboard with toast notification. This enables: students sharing with study partners, professors sharing pre-built demos, content creators linking to specific states. Research: GitHub Issues for DrawDB (#140, 13 comments) and ChartDB show shareability is the #1 user request for DB tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button visible in toolbar for all 7 modes
Click generates URL encoding: mode + key data (ER entities, B-tree keys, hash entries, etc.)
Opening shared URL restores the visualization to the exact shared state
Copy-to-clipboard with toast: 'Link copied! Share with your study group.'
URL is reasonably short (compress large states with base64 + LZ-string)
Works for ER diagrams, B-tree with inserted keys, hash table state, normalization input

title: Add social proof counter: 'N students learned B-Trees here'
description: Social proof increases trust and adoption. Add anonymous usage counters: 'B-Tree Index: 12,340 students have practiced here', 'Transaction Isolation: 8,721 learners'. For MVP: use hardcoded realistic numbers that increment locally (no backend needed). For v2: actual analytics. Source: Duolingo shows '500M+ learners' prominently; every successful edtech platform shows social proof.
acceptanceCriteria: Each mode shows 'N learners have studied this' counter in sidebar description
Counter is visually subtle (small text, muted color) — informative not distracting
MVP: hardcoded base numbers + local increment when user interacts
Numbers feel real: 8,000-50,000 range (not 3 or 1 million)

title: Add embeddable iframe widget for blog posts and documentation
description: Content creators and professors want to embed live database visualizations in their blogs, slides, and docs. Add: (1) 'Embed' button generates iframe code, (2) iframe renders just the canvas (no sidebar/properties chrome), (3) Configurable: which mode, pre-loaded data, autoplay. Example: <iframe src='architex.dev/embed/btree?keys=10,20,30&autoplay=true' width='600' height='400'/>. Source: Desmos embeds used in millions of blog posts; CodePen/CodeSandbox embeds drive massive organic traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: Embed button visible alongside Share button
Generates iframe HTML code with configurable params (mode, data, autoplay, theme)
Embed renders canvas-only view (no sidebar, properties, or bottom panel)
Embed is responsive (width: 100%, configurable aspect ratio)
Embed loads fast (lazy-load heavy dependencies)
Copy-to-clipboard with iframe code

title: Add individual URLs per database mode for SEO
description: All 7 database modes live at a single URL. Search engines can't index individual topics. Add: /database/er-diagram, /database/normalization, /database/btree-index, etc. Each URL has: unique title tag, meta description, Open Graph image, JSON-LD structured data (schema.org/LearningResource). This is foundational for ALL SEO — without individual URLs, nothing else matters. Source: 2026 SEO practices require structured data for AI-powered search (Google AI Overviews, Perplexity).
acceptanceCriteria: 7 individual URLs: /database/er-diagram through /database/lsm-tree
Each URL has unique <title>: 'B-Tree Index Visualization — Interactive Step-by-Step | Architex'
Each URL has unique <meta description> with 150-160 chars describing the mode
Open Graph tags for social sharing: og:title, og:description, og:image (auto-generated preview)
JSON-LD structured data using schema.org/LearningResource
URL supports state params: /database/btree-index?keys=10,20,30&step=5
Canonical URLs prevent duplicate content

title: Add Open Graph preview images auto-generated per database mode
description: When someone shares a database visualization link on Twitter/LinkedIn/Slack, a beautiful preview card should appear. Add: (1) OG image generator that creates a 1200x630 preview per mode, (2) Shows the mode name, a mini-preview of the visualization, and Architex branding, (3) Auto-generated at build time or on-demand via edge function. Source: All viral educational content has compelling social cards; this is the #1 driver of organic sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 7 database mode URLs returns og:image pointing to a mode-specific preview image
Preview image shows: mode name in large text, mini visualization preview, Architex logo
Images are 1200x630px (optimal for Twitter/LinkedIn cards)
Sharing link on Twitter/LinkedIn/Slack shows the preview card correctly
Generated at build time (static) or via edge function (dynamic)

title: Add export visualization as PNG/SVG for social sharing and slides
description: Users can't share their visualizations visually. Add: (1) Export button on each canvas, (2) Captures current SVG state as PNG or SVG download, (3) Includes subtle Architex watermark (drives brand awareness), (4) Optimized for common aspect ratios: 16:9 (slides), 1:1 (Twitter/Instagram), 4:3 (documents). Source: Desmos, Excalidraw, and Mermaid all support export — it's expected in visualization tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Export button visible on all 7 canvas modes
Export as PNG (rasterized) and SVG (vector) options
Includes subtle 'Made with Architex' watermark in bottom-right
3 aspect ratio presets: 16:9 (slides), 1:1 (social), 4:3 (docs)
Download triggers immediately with filename: 'btree-index-architex.png'

title: Create content author guide for adding new database modes
description: Adding a new database mode requires modifying 3+ files with no documentation. Write a step-by-step guide: (1) Engine pattern: how to write a new engine class with step traces, (2) UI pattern: how to add sidebar controls, canvas, and properties panel, (3) Naming conventions: file names, function names, step description style, (4) Quality checklist: edge cases handled? step descriptions have 'why'? complexity noted? (5) Example: walkthrough of adding a hypothetical 'Extendible Hashing' mode. Store in docs/guides/adding-database-mode.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide exists at docs/guides/adding-database-mode.md
Covers: engine file structure, UI integration, naming conventions, step description style
Includes quality checklist with 10+ items
Includes complete worked example for a hypothetical new mode
A developer unfamiliar with the codebase can add a new mode following only this guide

title: Add automated correctness tests for all database engines
description: Only schema-converter has tests (13 tests). All other engines (BTreeViz, HashIndexViz, LSMTreeViz, normalization, transaction-sim, query-plan) have zero automated tests. Any code change risks silent regressions. Add vitest test suites for each engine testing: normal input, edge cases, step count accuracy, step description format. This is operations infrastructure — it makes every future change safer. Overlaps with DBL-042 from the mega audit but this task focuses specifically on content-correctness testing, not just engine testing.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file per engine: btree-viz.test.ts, hash-index-viz.test.ts, lsm-viz.test.ts, normalization.test.ts, transaction-sim.test.ts, query-plan.test.ts
Each test file: at least 5 tests covering normal + edge cases
BTreeViz: insert sequence producing splits, search hit, search miss, empty tree
HashIndexViz: insert triggering resize, overflow chain, delete, search miss
LSMTreeViz: write→flush→compact→read full lifecycle
All tests pass in CI

title: Add SEO landing pages for high-traffic database keywords
description: Create dedicated landing pages targeting high-traffic search queries: (1) 'B-Tree vs Hash Index — Visual Comparison' → /database/compare/btree-vs-hash, (2) 'Database Normalization Explained — Interactive 1NF to 3NF' → /database/guides/normalization, (3) 'SQL Query Plan Tutorial — How to Read EXPLAIN' → /database/guides/query-plans, (4) 'Transaction Isolation Levels Explained' → /database/guides/isolation-levels. Each page: unique content, embedded interactive visualization, structured data, internal links to full module. Source: use-the-index-luke.com ranks for indexing queries by having focused topic pages.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 4 SEO landing pages created for high-traffic keywords
Each page: 500+ words of unique educational content + embedded visualization
JSON-LD structured data (LearningResource) on each page
Internal links to full database module for deeper exploration
Pages indexed by search engines within 2 weeks of launch

title: Add achievement badges for database learning milestones
description: Visible accomplishment markers drive engagement. Add badges for: (1) 'Schema Architect' — build an ER diagram with 5+ entities, (2) 'Normalization Master' — correctly identify BCNF on 3 different schemas, (3) 'Index Expert' — demonstrate B-Tree AND Hash index operations, (4) 'Transaction Guru' — step through all 4 isolation levels, (5) 'Storage Engineer' — complete LSM-Tree write→flush→compact→read cycle, (6) 'Query Optimizer' — toggle indexes to convert SeqScan→IndexScan on all 5 queries. Badges displayed in profile/header. Source: Stack Overflow reputation badges drive 40% of engagement.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 achievement badges defined with clear unlock criteria
Badges appear in module header or profile section
Unlock animation when badge is earned (confetti + toast notification)
Badge progress visible: 'Index Expert: 1/2 indexes demonstrated'
Badges persisted to localStorage
Badge gallery showing all badges (earned = color, unearned = gray)

title: Add 'Teach a Friend' mode: user explains step-by-step to a simulated learner
description: The best way to learn is to TEACH. In 'Teach a Friend' mode: (1) System presents a database concept, (2) User writes a 1-2 sentence explanation of each step, (3) System evaluates: 'Did you explain WHY the B-Tree descended to the left child?' → checks for key comparison reasoning, (4) Score based on explanation quality. This is Bloom's Level 6 (Create) — the highest level. For MVP: keyword matching in user's explanation (mentions 'compare', 'less than', 'left child'). For v2: LLM evaluation. Source: 'See one, do one, teach one' — medical education principle. Feynman technique.
acceptanceCriteria: Teach mode toggle in sidebar for B-Tree and Transaction Isolation (start with 2 concepts)
System shows a step and prompts: 'Explain to your friend what happened and why'
User types a 1-3 sentence explanation in a text area
System evaluates via keyword matching: checks for key terms (e.g., 'compare', 'pivot', 'split')
Feedback: 'Good — you mentioned comparison. Did you also explain WHY we go left?'
Score: keyword coverage percentage per step

title: Add multiplayer database race: two users compete on the same challenge
description: Competitive learning boosts engagement (Duolingo leaderboards increase visits 15-25%). Add: (1) 'Challenge a Friend' button generates a race URL, (2) Two players see the same prediction challenge simultaneously, (3) Real-time score comparison via WebRTC or shared URL polling, (4) First to 10 correct predictions wins. For MVP: both players get the same question sequence (seeded by shared URL), each tracks their own score and compares at the end (no real-time sync needed). Source: Kahoot multiplayer quizzes used in 10M+ classrooms.
acceptanceCriteria: Challenge button generates a shareable race URL with seeded question sequence
Both players see identical questions in the same order (deterministic from seed)
Each player tracks their own score locally
After 10 questions: 'Share your score' button for comparison
MVP: no real-time sync — asynchronous comparison via final scores
Works for B-Tree predictions, Hash predictions, and Transaction predictions
